issues.title: Notification timing contradictory in close-out.md Section 1
issues.details: The 'Ownership of commit / push / notification' subsection said all three actions 'run before invoking close_session', then the next paragraph correctly stated notification fires after close_session succeeds. The two readings contradict each other in the canonical contract document.
issues.location: ai-router/docs/close-out.md, Section 1 'Ownership of commit / push / notification'
issues.resolution: Split the bullet into two: (a) git commit/push run before close_session, (b) send_session_complete_notification runs after close_session returns succeeded. The previously-separate notification-ordering paragraph was folded into the second bullet so there is one canonical statement.

issues.title: Original proposal §3.3 items 4 and 6 still describe the superseded contract
issues.details: The post-implementation revision note at the top of the proposal flags those items as superseded, but a reader could land on §3.3 directly via a deep link / search and miss the revision note. Verifier recommended inline 'Superseded' markers at the items themselves.
issues.resolution: Inline '[Superseded by post-implementation revision above: ...]' markers added to both items so a reader who lands on §3.3 directly sees the corrected contract.
